**Secure API for LLM using FastAPI, JWT and OpenRouter**

**LLM Proxy API**

A REST API service for interacting with Large Language Models (LLMs) via **OpenRouter**, with user authentication, chat history storage, and session management.

The project is built with **FastAPI** and uses **JWT authentication**, **SQLite**, and a layered architecture inspired by **Clean Architecture** principles.

-----
**Features**

- User registration and authentication
- JWT-based authorization
- Sending prompts to a Large Language Model
- Chat history storage in database
- Retrieving chat history
- Clearing chat history
- Health check endpoint
-----
**Technologies**

- Python 3.11+
- FastAPI
- SQLAlchemy
- SQLite
- OpenRouter API
- Pydantic
- Uvicorn
- Ruff (linter)
-----




**Project Structure**

app\
├── api\
│   ├── deps.py\
│   ├── routes\_auth.py\
│   └── routes\_chat.py\
│\
├── core\
│   ├── config.py\
│   ├── errors.py\
│   └── security.py\
│\
├── db\
│   ├── base.py\
│   ├── models.py\
│   └── session.py\
│\
├── repositories\
│   ├── chat\_messages.py\
│   └── users.py\
│\
├── schemas\
│   ├── auth.py\
│   ├── chat.py\
│   └── user.py\
│\
├── services\
│   └── openrouter\_client.py\
│\
├── usecases\
│   ├── auth.py\
│   └── chat.py\
│\
└── main.py

![](data:image/png;base64,)

![](data:image/png;base64,)-----

**Installation and Setup**

Extract the project archive

Download and extract the ZIP archive containing the project.

Example:

llm-p.zip → extract → llm-p/

Open the project directory in the terminal.

-----
**Create a virtual environment**

uv venv

Activate it:

MacOS / Linux

source .venv/bin/activate

Windows

.venv\Scripts\activate

-----
**Install dependencies**

uv pip install -e .

-----
**Environment Variables**

Create a .env file in the project root:

APP\_NAME=llm-proxy\
ENV=dev\
\
JWT\_SECRET=supersecret\
JWT\_ALG=HS256\
ACCESS\_TOKEN\_EXPIRE\_MINUTES=60\
\
SQLITE\_PATH=./app.db\
\
OPENROUTER\_API\_KEY=your\_api\_key\_here\
OPENROUTER\_BASE\_URL=https://openrouter.ai/api/v1\
OPENROUTER\_MODEL=mistralai/mistral-7b-instruct\
OPENROUTER\_SITE\_URL=http://localhost:8000\
OPENROUTER\_APP\_NAME=llm-proxy

You can generate an API key at:

[**https://openrouter.ai/keys**](https://openrouter.ai/keys)

-----
**Running the Server**

Start the server:

uv run uvicorn app.main:app --reload

The API will be available at:

http://127.0.0.1:8000

![](data:image/png;base64,)-----

**API Documentation (Swagger)**

Interactive API documentation is available at:

http://127.0.0.1:8000/docs

![](data:image/png;base64,)-----

**Authentication**

**Register User**

POST /auth/register

Example request:

{\
`  `"email": "student\_surname@email.com",\
`  `"password": "password123"\
}

⚠️ Important

The email must follow the format:

student\_surname@email.com

![](data:image/png;base64,)

![](data:image/png;base64,)-----

**Login**

POST /auth/login

Uses **OAuth2 password flow**.

Example credentials:

username: student\_surname@email.com\
password: password123

Response example:

{\
`  `"access\_token": "JWT\_TOKEN",\
`  `"token\_type": "bearer"\
}

![](data:image/png;base64,)

![](data:image/png;base64,)-----

**Swagger Authorization**

To access protected endpoints:

1. Click **Authorize** in Swagger
1. Insert the token

Bearer <JWT\_TOKEN>

![](data:image/png;base64,)-----

**Chat API**

**Send Message to LLM**

POST /chat

Example request:

{\
`  `"prompt": "Explain FastAPI simply",\
`  `"temperature": 0.7\
}

Response example:

{\
`  `"answer": "FastAPI is a modern Python web framework..."\
}

![](data:image/png;base64,)-----

**Chat History**

GET /chat/history

Returns the conversation history stored in the database.

![](data:image/png;base64,)-----

**Delete Chat History**

DELETE /chat/history

Deletes all stored messages for the current user.

![](data:image/png;base64,)-----

**Health Check**

GET /health

Example response:

{\
`  `"status": "ok"\
}

-----
**Database**

The project uses **SQLite**.

The database file is created automatically:

app.db

To inspect the database:

sqlite3 app.db

![](data:image/png;base64,)-----

**Code Quality**

Run linter:

ruff check .

Format code:

ruff format .

![](data:image/png;base64,)-----

**Example Workflow**

1 Register a new user\
2 Login using /auth/login\
3 Receive a JWT token\
4 Authorize in Swagger\
5 Send prompts to /chat\
6 Check stored messages using /chat/history

-----
**Architecture**

The project is organized into multiple layers:

**API layer**\
Handles HTTP requests and responses.

**UseCases layer**\
Contains business logic of the application.

**Repositories layer**\
Handles database interactions.

**Services layer**\
Responsible for external API integrations (OpenRouter).

**Core layer**\
Contains configuration, security utilities, and shared components.

This structure follows simplified **Clean Architecture** principles.
